I could NOT independently verify that the live Railway/Vercel/Supabase infrastructure has actually been imported into a real TF state file in this backfill — that requires live cloud credentials and remote state access, which is out of scope for a static, credential-less attestation backfill. This sub-criterion is left honestly unattested rather than assumed true.
docs/operations/runbooks/railway-deploy.md and workers-runbook.md both present and reference the 3 workers

notes: RETROACTIVE ATTESTATION BACKFILL (ENG-OPS-002.R16, remediating GOV-A-001). No canonical attestation.json existed (only task-tracking.json). Primary delivery evidence: PR #291 'ci(infra): build + push the 3 worker images (INFRA-TF-005)' (merged 2026-06-06, commit 382b63a65d81d787fbcdad16a569b3a85c03678c) and PR #296 'docs(infra): terraform import runbook + import-blocks template (INFRA-TF-005)' (merged 2026-06-06, commit f7df61d0a3472100fe6104aa249924c6325d6268) — both have the task ID literally in the PR title, independently confirmed MERGED via `gh pr view`. PR #298 (gated railway-deploy workflow) and #297 (buildx retry fix) are related follow-on CI hardening. HONEST PARTIAL: two of the three DoD sub-criteria (CI coverage, runbooks) are solidly verified; the third (live infra actually imported into TF state with a clean plan) cannot be verified without live cloud/remote-state access from this static backfill session, so it is marked met=false in definition_of_done_items. Verdict set to PARTIAL (not COMPLETE) to reflect that one of three DoD criteria is unverified rather than confirmed — the documented, tooled import procedure (imports.tf.example + terraform-import.md) exists, but whether it was actually RUN against live state is a claim only a human with cloud access, or a future CI run, can confirm. Flagging for human follow-up rather than rounding up to COMPLETE.
